content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 17, 1991


Robert A. Sessions
Chief of Police
City of Barstow
220 East Mountain View Street
Barstow, CA 92311-9981

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-91-221

Dear Mr. Sessions:

You have requested advice concerning your reporting
obligations under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

As the chief of police for the City of Barstow, are you
required to disclose on your statement of economic interests your
advisory board positions with the Desert Community Bank and the
San Bernardino County Central Credit Union?

CONCLUSION

At this time there is no obligation for you to report any
business positions on the statement of economic interests. This
advice may change once the City of Barstow corrects its conflict
of interest code (see analysis below).


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Page Two
May 17, 1991

FACTS

As the chief of police for the city of Barstow, you are
designated in the city's conflict of interest code to file a
Statement of Economic Interests Form 730. The city's conflict of
interest code requires you to disclose on schedules A, D, F and G.

Schedule G is a business position schedule. You are a member
of an advisory board to the Desert Community Bank and to the San
Bernardino County Central Credit Union. You are asking if the
advisory board positions are required to be disclosed on your
Statement of Economic Interests.

ANALYSIS

The Political Reform Act requires every agency to adopt and
promulgate a conflict of interest code. (Section 87300.) A
conflict of interest code is a document which designates the
positions within the agency which make or participate in the
making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect
on any financial interest and further sets forth the types of
investments, business positions, income and real property made
reportable by those designated employees. (Section 87302,
emphasis added.)

The responsibility for determining if a code meets the
specifications of the Act rests with the "code reviewing body"
who, in this instance, is the Barstow City Council. (Section
87303.) For this reason the Commission can only provide informal
assistance.2/

The conflict of interest code for the City of Barstow does
not set forth the specific types of investments, business
positions, income and real property which are reportable by the
positions designated in the code. The code sets out schedules
which the city has determined should be completed but fails to set
forth the types of investments, business positions, income or real
property to be reported on those schedules. The code for the City
of Barstow does not meet the requirements of Section 87302.

For example, a properly formulated code might require the
disclosure of investments and business positions in business
entities, and income from sources which manufacture or supply
equipment, machinery, supplies, or services of the type used by

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

Page Three
May 17, 1991


the city. A person assigned this category would disclose his or
her investments in such an entity on schedules A and C-2; business
positions on schedule G and income on schedules D, E, F and H.

The city attorney's office will receive a copy of this letter
and be apprised of this error in the code. Once the code is
corrected, should it require the disclosure of investments and
business positions in business entities, and income from sources
which are banks or lending institutions, we would have to
reexamine this issue to determine if your advisory board position
constitutes a position of management and should be reportable.
Until that time, it does not appear that you need to disclose any
business positions.

I hope this information has been helpful to you. If I can be
of further assistance please feel free to call me at (916) 322-
5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division

SH/JET/jt
cc. City Attorneys Office
City of Barstow
